mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns when policy is actively easing. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as growth support rather than a late inflation response.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls at least 0.25 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing episodes do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the sign turns negative when CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and slower household income growth, which increases recession risk and depresses equity prices. When unemployment jumps from a low base, the market should underperform over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice earnings risk faster than macro data stabilizes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-triggered episodes do not have below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if PAYEMS growth offsets the unemployment shock and the signal disappears for more than two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS reflects weakening nominal consumer spending and softer revenue growth for cyclicals, so the S&P 500 should deliver negative 20-day forward returns when retail sales momentum turns down. The effect is most visible when GDP growth is also soft, because sales weakness then signals economy-wide demand deceleration rather than transitory category mix.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls for 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive retail-sales declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal vanishes when GDPC1 annualized growth is below trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO indicates a supply-side production slowdown that usually compresses corporate margins and raises recession odds, which should weigh on the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when production weakness is purely idiosyncratic and labor data remain strong, because then earnings expectations do not reprice broadly.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if large INDPRO declines are not followed by weaker 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is neutral when PAYEMS growth stays positive and stable.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling HOUST and rising UNRATE identifies a synchronized housing and labor slowdown that should depress risk appetite and forward earnings expectations. This interaction is more informative than either series alone because housing weakness matters most when it coincides with labor market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-UNRATE deterioration does not predict below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if either variable alone explains the effect as well as the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains far below inflation, real policy stays negative and the market tends to suffer valuation compression and duration pressure. The effect should be positive only after inflation peaks and FEDFUNDS starts catching up; before that, higher CPIAUCSL is bearish for 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains negative after FEDFUNDS overtakes CPIAUCSL growth.

mechanism_narrative: A steady decline in PAYEMS signals labor-income expansion and supports consumption, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 60 trading days as earnings estimates firm. The trend is most reliable when UNRATE is also falling, because payroll gains then reflect broad-based demand strength rather than noisy job churn.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained payroll gains do not lead to above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips negative when unemployment is also rising.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in HOUST combined with rising RSAFS identifies a synchronized cyclical demand recovery, which should lift the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days through improved revenue expectations and sentiment. The signal fails if housing rises alone without retail-sales acceleration, because then the recovery is too narrow to support broad equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months and RSAFS increases month over month
failure_condition: Reject if joint housing and retail-strength episodes do not outperform baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive HOUST without RSAFS growth performs equally well.
